mechanism_narrative: A declining FEDFUNDS rate reduces discount rates and eases financial conditions, so lower policy rates predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is falsified if rate cuts do not precede higher 20-day index returns.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS declines over a 1-month window but SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in most triggered observations, or rolling Rank IC stays below 0.03 for 2 years, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL rises faster than the prior 3-month pace, inflation pressure forces tighter real rates and multiple compression, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation acceleration > 0.3pp versus its prior 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if CPIAUCSL acceleration above the trigger is followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the negative sign is not stable across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE combined with slowing PAYEMS indicates labor-market deterioration that weakens consumption expectations and raises recession risk, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of higher UNRATE and lower PAYEMS does not improve negative-return frequency versus either series alone, or if 60-day forward returns are not below baseline in triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly rise in RSAFS above its recent trend signals stronger consumer demand and revenue momentum, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS monthly growth > 0.5% and above its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if RSAFS growth above the trigger is not followed by above-median 20-day SPX returns, or if the sign flips negative in more than one rolling 2-year segment.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in INDPRO with stable CPIAUCSL indicates real output gains without immediate inflation pressure, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days through better earnings expectations.
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO momentum with stable CPI does not outperform unconditional 5-day SPX returns, or if the interaction term is not significant in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A collapse in HOUST signals weakening housing demand and tighter cyclical growth expectations, which should predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its 6-month average by more than 8%
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST drops below the trigger yet 60-day SPX returns are positive in most cases, or if the signal has no lift over baseline in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening cycle, a high FEDFUNDS level above recent history should compress equity valuations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect should be strongest when CPIAUCSL is also elevated.
failure_condition: Reject if FEDFUNDS above the trigger does not precede negative 20-day SPX returns on average, or if the negative effect disappears when CPIAUCSL is elevated.

mechanism_narrative: A falling PAYEMS count together with rising UNRATE captures weakening labor income and consumer stress, which should be followed by negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal fails when job losses are offset by faster industrial growth.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint deterioration in PAYEMS and UNRATE does not produce negative 20-day SPX returns more often than chance, or if adding INDPRO does not improve the hit rate in walk-forward evaluation.
